name: Basil Sunflower Seed Pesto
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Coarsely chopped fresh basil leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender or food processor, puree the basil with the sunflower seeds, olive oil, Parmesan, butter, and garlic in batches.
2. Add salt to taste.
3. Transfer the pesto to a small bowl.
4. Cover the surface of the pesto with plastic wrap to prevent discoloration.
5. The pesto will keep, covered and chilled, for 2 weeks.
6. To use: For every pound of dried pasta cooking in a pot of boiling salted water, stir together in a heated serving bowl 3/4 cup pesto and 2/3 cup hot pasta cooking water.
7. When the pasta is al dente, drain it in a colander, add it to the pesto mixture, and toss with lemon juice, salt, and pepper to taste.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat," "press" instead of "smash," and avoid words like "destroy," "burn," "splutter," or "execute."
